title: 1.2: States that the wcswidth_step function in kitty/wcswidth.c handles character width calculation by processing characters sequentially
1.5: States that when ZWJ emoji sequences exceed available space, the terminal wraps content across lines rather than keeping the sequence as a single unit
1.6: States that combining characters are stored in the cc_idx array of the CPUCell structure (e.g., up to 3 combining character indices)
1.8: States that control sequence state queries use DECRQSS mechanism via screen_request_capabilities function in kitty/screen.c
1.9: States that kitty follows a display-oriented approach preserving exact codepoint sequences rather than strict NFC or NFD normalization
1.11: Reports runtime test output with actual cursor positions and cell widths (e.g., cursor x=8 for family emoji, cursor x=1 for VS15)
